1. A 25-year-old woman at 8 weeks gestation presents for her first prenatal visit. She has no prior pregnancies. Vital signs are normal. Physical exam is unremarkable. Urinalysis shows no abnormalities. What is the most appropriate next step?
a. Schedule routine prenatal care and return visits
b. Administer methotrexate
c. Perform dilation and curettage
d. Order immediate laparoscopy
e. Recommend termination

2. A 55-year-old man with a 40-pack-year smoking history presents with a 6-month history of progressive dyspnea on exertion and chronic productive cough. Pulmonary function tests show FEV1/FVC ratio of 0.62 and FEV1 of 58% predicted. Chest X-ray shows hyperinflation and flattened diaphragms. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
a. Asthma
b. Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
c. Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
d. Pulmonary embolism
e. Congestive heart failure

Answer Key:
1. A
2. C
